 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ef4","title":"[PACK] dns.route53 - AWS Route53 Pack","description":"# AWS Route53 DNS Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for AWS Route53 DNS operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `aws route53 delete-hosted-zone`
- `aws route53 change-resource-record-sets` with DELETE action
- `aws route53 delete-health-check`
- `aws route53 delete-query-logging-config`
- `aws route53 delete-traffic-policy`
- `aws route53 delete-reusable-delegation-set`

## Safe Patterns
- `aws route53 list-hosted-zones`
- `aws route53 list-resource-record-sets`
- `aws route53 get-hosted-zone`
- `aws route53 test-dns-answer`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: dns.* (git_safety_guard-x3g)
- Keywords: aws, route53
- Note: change-resource-record-sets with DELETE action needs detection

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Zone deletion blocked
- [ ] Record DELETE action blocked
- [ ] Health check deletion blocked
- [ ] Tests cover common Route53 operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:33:50.22747334-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:28:46.289172988-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:28:46.289172988-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ef4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.419052634-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ef4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.462736536-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ef4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.5061134-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ef4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.550875661-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ef4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.595335162-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ef4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.637870834-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ef4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.367150624-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ef4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.413060291-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ehc","title":"Implement GitLab CI extractor (.gitlab-ci.yml)","description":"## Objective

Implement an extractor for GitLab CI YAML files to detect executable command contexts.

## File Pattern

- `.gitlab-ci.yml`
- `*.gitlab-ci.yml` (includes)

## Extraction Targets

### Script Blocks (executed)
- `script:` arrays
- `before_script:` arrays
- `after_script:` arrays

### NOT Extracted (data only)
- `variables:` values
- `rules:` conditions
- `only:`/`except:` patterns
- Comments

## Example

```yaml
deploy:
  script:
    - kubectl delete namespace prod  # EXTRACT THIS
  variables:
    DANGEROUS: "rm -rf /"  # DO NOT extract
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Correctly extracts script blocks with line numbers
- Ignores variable definitions and comments
- Handles YAML anchors and aliases
- Unit tests with representative fixtures","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Extract only from executed job script contexts (e.g., `script:` sections), not from:
  - variables
  - comments
  - descriptive fields
- Include negative fixtures covering “dangerous substring in docs/variables” cases.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:57.250174884-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T03:22:37.165219089-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T03:22:37.165219089-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented GitLab CI extractor in scan.rs (script/before_script/after_script, anchors/aliases, block scalars) + unit tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ehc","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:20.517428749-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ehc","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:46:22.595864802-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ekc","title":"[CATEGORY] featureflags.* - Feature Flag Pack Suite","description":"# Feature Flag Pack Suite

## Overview
Feature flags control feature rollout and experiments. Flag deletion can unexpectedly enable/disable features in production. Misconfiguration affects user experience.

## Packs to Implement

### featureflags.launchdarkly
- **Why Critical**: LaunchDarkly is the leading feature flag platform. Flag deletion affects all environments.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `ldcli flags delete`
  - `ldcli projects delete`
  - `ldcli environments delete`
  - API DELETE calls
  - Archiving flags (soft delete)
- **Safe Patterns**: `list`, `get`, `show`

### featureflags.split
- **Why Critical**: Split.io handles feature flags and experimentation.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - Split definition deletion
  - Environment deletion
  - Traffic type deletion
- **Safe Patterns**: `list`, `get`

### featureflags.flipt
- **Why Critical**: Open-source feature flag solution.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `flipt flag delete`
  - `flipt segment delete`
  - Database operations on Flipt DB
- **Safe Patterns**: `list`, `get`, `evaluate`

### featureflags.unleash
- **Why Critical**: Open-source feature management.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `unleash features delete`
  - `unleash projects delete`
  - API DELETE calls
- **Safe Patterns**: `list`, `get`

## Rationale
Feature flags often control critical functionality. Deleting a flag that defaults to 'off' can suddenly expose unfinished features. Deleting a flag that defaults to 'on' can break features users depend on.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 3 Epic (git_safety_guard-9ic)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Major feature flag platforms covered
- [ ] Flag deletion detected across environments
- [ ] Project/environment deletion handled
- [ ] Tests cover common flag operations","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:30:07.932792422-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T14:30:07.932792422-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ekc","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:13:10.863829813-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ekc","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ew0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:13:10.899739851-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ekc","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qff","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:13:10.933597519-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ekc","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-lrz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:13:10.96750511-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ekc","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:48.373220333-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ekc","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:48.416683369-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ekc","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:48.46179086-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ekc","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:48.509132654-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ekc","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:48.555902652-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ekc","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:48.601562272-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-erux","title":"Design user-friendly error messages for heredoc blocks","description":"# User-Friendly Error Messages for Heredoc Blocking

## Objective

When a heredoc command is blocked, provide clear, actionable error messages that help users understand WHY it was blocked and HOW to proceed.

## Current Error Format (for reference)

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║  ⚠️  BLOCKED: Destructive Command Detected                   ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  Command: git reset --hard HEAD~5                            ║
║  Reason:  Hard reset can permanently lose commits            ║
║  Pack:    core.git                                           ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

## Enhanced Error Format for Heredocs

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║  ⚠️  BLOCKED: Destructive Pattern in Heredoc                 ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  Language: Python                                            ║
║  Pattern:  os.system($CMD)                                   ║
║  Matched:  os.system("rm -rf /tmp/data")                    ║
║  Line:     3 of heredoc                                      ║
║  Reason:   Shell command execution via os.system             ║
║  Pack:     heredoc.python                                    ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  Context:                                                    ║
║    1│ import os                                              ║
║    2│ path = "/tmp/data"                                    ║
║  ▶ 3│ os.system("rm -rf /tmp/data")  ← BLOCKED             ║
║    4│ print("done")                                         ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  💡 Suggestions:                                             ║
║  • Use pathlib.Path.unlink() for single file deletion       ║
║  • Use shutil.rmtree() with explicit path validation        ║
║  • If intentional, use dcg allow --reason "..."             ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

## Error Message Components

### 1. Header

- Clear indication this is a BLOCK, not just a warning
- Specify it's a heredoc/inline script detection

### 2. Detection Details

- Language detected
- Pattern name that matched
- Actual matched text (truncated if long)
- Line number within heredoc
- Human-readable reason

### 3. Context Window

- Show 2-3 lines before and after the match
- Highlight the offending line with arrow
- Syntax highlighting if terminal supports it

### 4. Suggestions

- Language-specific safe alternatives
- How to override if intentional
- Link to documentation

## JSON Output Format

For Claude Code hook protocol:

```json
{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "permissionDecision": "deny",
    "reason": "Heredoc contains destructive pattern: os.system with shell command",
    "details": {
      "detection_type": "heredoc",
      "language": "python",
      "pattern": "os.system($CMD)",
      "matched_text": "os.system(\"rm -rf /tmp/data\")",
      "line_in_heredoc": 3,
      "pack": "heredoc.python",
      "suggestions": [
        "Use pathlib.Path.unlink() for single file deletion",
        "Use shutil.rmtree() with explicit path validation"
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

## Suggestions Database

Maintain per-pattern suggestions:

```rust
static PATTERN_SUGGESTIONS: &[(&str, &[&str])] = &[
    ("os.system", &[
        "Use subprocess.run() with shell=False",
        "Use pathlib for file operations",
    ]),
    ("shutil.rmtree", &[
        "Validate path is within expected directory",
        "Use explicit allowlist of deletable paths",
    ]),
];
```

## Test Cases

- Verify all pattern types have suggestions
- Verify context window is correctly extracted
- Verify line numbers are accurate
- Verify JSON output is valid
- Verify color output is readable
- Verify truncation for long matches

## Acceptance Criteria

- When heredoc detection blocks, stderr output includes:
  - language
  - stable rule ID (pack_id:pattern_name)
  - a small context window with highlighted line
  - actionable next steps (explain/allowlist)
- Hook JSON denial remains valid and includes enough structured details for tooling.
- Output is safe for non-TTY environments (no ANSI when not a TTY).
- Unit tests cover at least:
  - context window extraction
  - truncation
  - JSON schema validity

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ewbq","title":"[LIFECYCLE] Pack Maintenance and CLI Evolution Strategy","description":"# Pack Maintenance and CLI Evolution Strategy

## Problem
CLI tools evolve over time:
- New commands are added
- Command syntax changes
- Flags are deprecated or renamed
- New destructive operations emerge

We need a strategy to keep packs current and handle breaking changes.

## Maintenance Processes

### 1. CLI Tool Version Tracking
Create `docs/cli-versions.yaml`:
```yaml
secrets.vault:
  tool: vault
  tested_versions: ['1.15.x', '1.16.x']
  last_verified: 2024-01-15
  changelog_url: https://github.com/hashicorp/vault/releases
  
backup.rclone:
  tool: rclone
  tested_versions: ['1.65.x', '1.66.x']
  last_verified: 2024-01-10
  changelog_url: https://rclone.org/changelog/
```

### 2. Quarterly Version Audit
Scheduled task every quarter:
- Check each CLI tool for new releases
- Review changelogs for new destructive commands
- Update pack patterns if needed
- Update tested_versions
- Run full test suite against new versions

### 3. Breaking Change Protocol
When a CLI tool makes breaking changes:
1. Create urgent patch task
2. Update patterns for new syntax
3. Keep backward compatibility for N-1 version
4. Document in pack changelog
5. Announce in release notes

### 4. Deprecation Strategy
When removing patterns:
1. Mark as deprecated in code comments
2. Log warning when deprecated pattern matches
3. Keep deprecated pattern for 2 releases
4. Remove and document in changelog

### 5. User-Reported Updates
Process for handling user reports:
- New destructive command not covered
- False positive on new CLI version
- Pattern not matching expected command

## Automation

### CLI Version Checker
Create `scripts/check_cli_versions.sh`:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Runs in CI weekly to detect new CLI releases
# Opens GitHub issue if new version detected
```

### Pattern Compatibility Tests
Test patterns against multiple CLI versions in CI:
```yaml
matrix:
  vault_version: ['1.15.0', '1.16.0', 'latest']
  rclone_version: ['1.65.0', '1.66.0', 'latest']
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CLI version tracking file created
- [ ] Quarterly audit process documented
- [ ] Breaking change protocol defined
